entries.source:
Chat Edit: 'Setup a concrete plan that can reverse engineer having to manually create 50+ todos for wiring of 

features -> framework -> kernel 
help menu correctness correct file explorer correct docks correct settings correct flow for navigation via autonomous or agenticly correct win32 correct reversal and hotpatching correct LLM aka POSH 

What I need know is a 100% real from the very end of this where it will be the "last" feature to the very start which is the applications that we havent even got to use or see yet. Thats how the wiring and menus and flows need to go so when you open it it goes from the application back to the last feature and then continues but never in a circle or loop

So Framework and Menu Creating / Breadcrumb Drop Downs (File Edit Selection View Go Run Terminal Help Command Pallate Area Open Chat Open Quick Chat New Chat Editor New Chat Window Configure Inline Suggestions Manage Chat )

Its CRUCIAL that these also arent placeholders, make sure it doesnt jkust create a full scaffold of nothing, because thats not what it should do. It needs to make the actual production ready code regardless if the source is ready or not for it, regardless if the source will compile cleanly at that time or not, it doesnt matter, we can always come back, like usual, reverse engineering correctly, everything will line up correct instead of magically. The menus and breadcrumbs need to actually have the ability to right click so you can view the folder the source file is in or copy its path or save it because ctrl+s isnt working. It has to have setings menu and options/toggles for 100%! One thing leads to another, not guaranteed after twice.. Example File - New File etcetra!

Okay but these wiring dialogs should be the same for everything regardless of cli or gui '
